terminal/ghostty.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Rich TUI baselines. Ranks #2 of 8 tested terminals. Missing: CUP with DECOM, Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Sixel graphics, Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337), Capability Reporting (OSC 1337), Kitty graphics protocol.

terminal/kitty.analysis: Kitty scores 90% (132/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #7 of 8 tested terminals. Missing 14 features.

terminal/iterm2.analysis: iTerm2 scores 98% (144/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Rich TUI baselines. Ranks #1 of 8 tested terminals. Uniquely supports: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337), Kitty graphics protocol. Missing: CUP with DECOM, Sixel graphics, Capability Reporting (OSC 1337).

terminal/terminal-app.analysis: Terminal.app scores 90% (132/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #8 of 8 tested terminals. Missing 14 features.

terminal/warp.analysis: Warp scores 93% (124/134) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #6 of 8 tested terminals. Missing 9 features.

terminal/cursor.analysis: Cursor scores 93% (125/134) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #5 of 8 tested terminals. Missing: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols), Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation), Kitty keyboard protocol, Sixel graphics, Grapheme Cluster Cursor Movement, Kitty graphics protocol.

terminal/vs-code.analysis: VS Code scores 94% (126/134) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #3 of 8 tested terminals. Missing: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols), Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation), Sixel graphics, Grapheme Cluster Cursor Movement, Kitty graphics protocol.

baseline/core.analysis: 4 of 8 tested terminals achieve 100% Core TUI compliance. Lagging: Ghostty (99%), Kitty (99%), iTerm2 (99%). Most commonly missing: CUP with DECOM.

baseline/modern.analysis: 5 of 8 tested terminals achieve 100% Modern TUI compliance. Lagging: Kitty (96%), Terminal.app (96%), Warp (98%). Most commonly missing: Mode Reporting (DECRPM), Origin mode (DECOM).

baseline/rich.analysis: 0 of 8 tested terminals achieve 100% Rich TUI compliance. Lagging: Kitty (60%), Terminal.app (60%), Cursor (67%). Most commonly missing: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Kitty graphics protocol, XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo).

baseline/unicode.analysis: 2 of 8 tested terminals achieve 100% Unicode compliance. Lagging: Kitty (63%), Terminal.app (63%), Warp (63%). Most commonly missing: Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols), Grapheme Cluster Cursor Movement.

compare/ghostty-vs-kitty.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/147) vs Kitty at 90% (132/147). Ghostty leads by 5 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Mode Reporting (DECRPM), DECRQSS (Request Setting), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo), Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) and 4 more. Only in Kitty: Sixel graphics.

compare/ghostty-vs-iterm2.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/147) vs iTerm2 at 98% (144/147). iTerm2 leads by 3 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337), Kitty graphics protocol.

compare/ghostty-vs-warp.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/147) vs Warp at 93% (124/134). Ghostty leads by 2 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: CUP at screen boundaries, CUU stops at top, CUD stops at bottom, Mode Reporting (DECRPM), DECRQSS (Request Setting) and 11 more.

compare/ghostty-vs-terminal-app.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/147) vs Terminal.app at 90% (132/147). Ghostty leads by 5 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Mode Reporting (DECRPM), DECRQSS (Request Setting), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo), Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) and 4 more. Only in Terminal.app: Sixel graphics.

compare/iterm2-vs-kitty.analysis: iTerm2 scores 98% (144/147) vs Kitty at 90% (132/147). iTerm2 leads by 8 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Mode Reporting (DECRPM), DECRQSS (Request Setting), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo), Terminal Version (XTVERSION) and 7 more. Only in Kitty: Sixel graphics.

compare/kitty-vs-warp.analysis: Kitty scores 90% (132/147) vs Warp at 93% (124/134). Warp leads by 3 percentage points. Only in Kitty: CUP at screen boundaries, CUU stops at top, CUD stops at bottom, EL erases with bg color, ED at scroll region boundary and 5 more. Only in Warp: Origin mode (DECOM), Kitty keyboard protocol.

compare/iterm2-vs-terminal-app.analysis: iTerm2 scores 98% (144/147) vs Terminal.app at 90% (132/147). iTerm2 leads by 8 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Mode Reporting (DECRPM), DECRQSS (Request Setting), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo), Terminal Version (XTVERSION) and 7 more. Only in Terminal.app: Sixel graphics.

compare/iterm2-vs-warp.analysis: iTerm2 scores 98% (144/147) vs Warp at 93% (124/134). iTerm2 leads by 5 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: CUP at screen boundaries, CUU stops at top, CUD stops at bottom, Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Mode Reporting (DECRPM) and 14 more.

compare/cursor-vs-vs-code.analysis: Cursor scores 93% (125/134) vs VS Code at 94% (126/134). VS Code leads by 1 percentage point. Only in VS Code: Kitty keyboard protocol.

compare/terminal-app-vs-warp.analysis: Terminal.app scores 90% (132/147) vs Warp at 93% (124/134). Warp leads by 3 percentage points. Only in Terminal.app: CUP at screen boundaries, CUU stops at top, CUD stops at bottom, EL erases with bg color, ED at scroll region boundary and 5 more. Only in Warp: Origin mode (DECOM), Kitty keyboard protocol.

sgr.analysis: The SGR (Text Styling) category covers 29 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

cursor.analysis: The Cursor category covers 17 features. Top performers (100%): Cursor, Warp, Cursor, VS Code. Common gaps: CUP with DECOM (4 terminals fail).

text.analysis: The Text category covers 16 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2. Common gaps: Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation) (6 terminals fail), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) (5 terminals fail), Emoji wide chars (2 cols) (1 terminal fails).

erase.analysis: The Erase category covers 11 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

editing.analysis: The Editing category covers 5 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

modes.analysis: The Modes category covers 15 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, Warp, Cursor, VS Code and 1 more. Common gaps: Origin mode (DECOM) (2 terminals fail).

scrollback.analysis: The Scrollback category covers 9 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

reset.analysis: The Reset category covers 4 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

extensions.analysis: The Extensions category covers 18 features. Common gaps: Kitty graphics protocol (7 terminals fail), Sixel graphics (6 terminals fail), Capability Reporting (OSC 1337) (4 terminals fail).

charsets.analysis: The Character Sets category covers 4 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

device.analysis: The Device Status category covers 9 features. Top performers (100%): iTerm2. Common gaps: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3) (7 terminals fail), XTGETTCAP (Query Terminfo) (6 terminals fail), DECRQSS (Request Setting) (3 terminals fail).

input.analysis: The Input Protocols category covers 6 features. All 8 tested terminals achieve 100%.

unicode.analysis: Unicode defines 10 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 78%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2. Lowest: Cursor at 70% (7/10).

ecma-48.analysis: ECMA-48 Standard defines 60 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 100%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Cursor and 3 more.

vt100.analysis: VT100 defines 50 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 99%. Full compliance (100%): Cursor, Warp, Cursor, VS Code. Lowest: Terminal.app at 96% (48/50).

vt220.analysis: VT220 defines 4 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 100%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 3 more.

vt510.analysis: VT510 defines 10 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 84%. Full compliance (100%): iTerm2. Lowest: Warp at 70% (7/10).

dec-private-modes.analysis: DEC Private Modes defines 22 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 97%. Full compliance (100%): Cursor, Warp, Cursor, VS Code. Lowest: Terminal.app at 91% (20/22).

xterm-extensions.analysis: Xterm Extensions defines 33 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 97%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2. Lowest: Terminal.app at 94% (31/33).

kitty-extensions.analysis: Kitty Extensions defines 6 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 77%. Full compliance (100%): iTerm2. Lowest: Cursor at 67% (4/6).

osc.analysis: Operating System Commands (OSC) defines 13 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 92%. Full compliance (100%): Warp, Cursor, VS Code. Lowest: Terminal.app at 85% (11/13).

sixel.analysis: Sixel Graphics defines 1 feature in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 25%. Full compliance (100%): Kitty, Terminal.app. Lowest: Cursor at 0% (0/1).

Other frameworks target: Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui (Modern TUI).

framework/textual.analysis: Textual requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 8 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor. Partial compatibility: Kitty (96%), Terminal.app (96%), Warp (98%). Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Bubbletea, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/bubbletea.analysis: Bubbletea requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 8 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor. Partial compatibility: Kitty (96%), Terminal.app (96%), Warp (98%). Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Textual, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/ratatui.analysis: Ratatui requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 8 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor. Partial compatibility: Kitty (96%), Terminal.app (96%), Warp (98%). Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/blessed.analysis: Blessed requires the Core TUI baseline — 4 of 8 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Warp, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor. Partial compatibility: Ghostty (99%), Kitty (99%), iTerm2 (99%), Terminal.app (99%). Same baseline tier (Core TUI) as Ink. Other frameworks target: Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui (Modern TUI).
